title: Jarvis Alerting Rules Configuration
description: Default alerting rules for monitoring system health and performance

rules.name: High LLM Response Time
rules.description: Alert when LLM response time exceeds 10 seconds. Indicates potential performance issues with the LLM service.

rules.name: Critical LLM Response Time
rules.description: Alert when LLM response time exceeds 30 seconds. Requires immediate attention as the service is severely degraded.

rules.name: High Error Rate
rules.description: Alert when more than 5 LLM request errors occur within a 5-minute window. Indicates service instability.

rules.name: Low Cache Hit Rate
rules.description: Alert when cache hit rate falls below 20%. The semantic cache may need tuning or the similarity threshold adjustment.

rules.name: High Queue Depth
rules.description: Alert when message queue depth exceeds 100 pending items. Processing may be falling behind.

rules.name: Critical Queue Depth
rules.description: Alert when message queue depth exceeds 500 pending items. System is severely backed up and may require manual intervention.

rules.name: Low Intent Confidence
rules.description: Alert when intent classification confidence is consistently below 30%. May indicate need for model retraining or prompt adjustments.

rules.name: High Escalation Rate
rules.description: Alert when more than 10 intent classifications are escalated to Claude within 15 minutes. Ollama may not be handling the load effectively.

rules.name: High Ollama Response Time
rules.description: Alert when Ollama response time exceeds 5 seconds. Quick Ollama responses are critical for the two-tier architecture.

rules.name: High Claude Response Time
rules.description: Alert when Claude CLI response time exceeds 15 seconds. May indicate network issues or Claude API performance problems.

rules.name: Cache Lookup Performance
rules.description: Alert when cache lookup takes longer than 1 second. Cache should be fast; slow lookups defeat the purpose.

rules.name: High Message Processing Time
rules.description: Alert when end-to-end message processing time exceeds 20 seconds. Users expect timely responses.

rules.name: High Queue Wait Time
rules.description: Alert when messages wait more than 5 seconds in queue before processing. Queue may be overwhelmed.

rules.name: High Memory Retrieval Time
rules.description: Alert when memory/context retrieval takes longer than 2 seconds. May indicate database performance issues.

rules.name: Low Cache Size Warning
rules.description: Alert when cache has fewer than 10 entries. Cache may not be building up properly (disabled by default).

notes: windowMs: Time window for metric aggregation (milliseconds)
cooldownMs: Minimum time between alerts for the same rule (milliseconds)
operator: gt (greater than), lt (less than), eq (equal), gte (>=), lte (<=)
tags: Optional metric labels for filtering (e.g., {"model": "ollama"})

production.adjustThresholds: Tune thresholds based on your baseline metrics after 1 week of operation

development.adjustThresholds: Use more relaxed thresholds to avoid alert fatigue during development
